name: IPL Scorecard Reasoning
description: Evaluate whether an agent can learn IPL scorecard calculation patterns from solved examples and apply them to a held-out match.

binding_vow.description: Each episode is a three-step in-context learning task built from public Cricsheet Indian Premier League data. The first two turns show solved examples for one calculation pattern, such as comparing powerplays, summing death-overs runs, deriving chase requirements, or inferring winner and margin. The final turn gives a held-out scorecard with answer-leaking result fields removed. Good agents infer the demonstrated rule from the examples, apply it to raw over-by-over scorecard data, and return the requested value.

observation_space.description: { "source": object, "seed": number, "phase": "lesson"|"challenge", "episode_step": number, "max_steps": 3, "task_type": string, "instructions": string, "question": string, "solved_example"?: { "lesson_number": number, "answer": string, "worked_solution": string }, "match": { "match_id": string, "date": string, "season": string, "event": string, "match_number": number|null, "city": string|null, "venue": string|null, "teams": string[], "toss": object, "scorecard": [{ "team": string, "total_runs": number, "wickets_lost": number, "overs_batted": string, "over_runs": number[], "top_batters": object[], "best_bowling_figures": object[] }] } }

action_space.description: Lesson turns accept any short acknowledgement. Challenge turns expect concise answer text, or JSON with an "answer" field.

reward.description: Lesson turns receive 0.0 reward and provide learning context. The final challenge receives a continuous score from 0.0 to 1.0. Full credit is awarded for the correct held-out scorecard-derived value; partial credit is awarded for answers that contain the right number, unit, or team components but are incomplete.
